lines: Tomas watches the road through a broken shutter.
"No boot marks since before the knives. Not Remnant boots, not wall boots. Something swallowed the road before the cult found us."

choices.label: Ask why he listened to Selka
Ask what he would do next

lines: Tomas has the look of a man who counted exits until the numbers stopped helping.
"I ran messages to the Hallowfen outposts on the same route every week. Then the bells quit answering, and Selka put one finger in my face until I agreed not to go alone."
"The route is simple if nothing wants you. Chapel gate, dry ditch, broken toll post, then the wall road."
"Simple routes kill more people than complicated ones. A man stops watching because the ground has behaved itself before."
"Bells are lazy things. They only have one job, and they still make men pull ropes for them."
"When every tower goes quiet at once, do not blame laziness. Blame hands, blades, or something worse than either."
"If I knew, I would be more useful and less frightened. Selka says those two states rarely meet in the same man."
"The cult wanted the road quiet. I know that much. The part that scares me is whether they found it quiet when they arrived."
"Because Selka has a voice that can make a grown man feel like an unpaid child."
"Also because Selka was right, which you will not repeat unless you enjoy feeding her."
"Cruel work, cult-breaker. I knew I should have charged for that confession."
"Runner. Scouts get better boots and worse orders."
"I carried notices, ration marks, warning scraps. Half the job was arriving before the lie did."

choices.label: Ask what he would do next

lines: "If Selka lets me, I watch the road by daylight and mark what is missing."
"If she does not, I will watch it anyway from somewhere she cannot see. I remain a fool. I am only becoming a supervised one."
